invalid_review_attempts.reason: Valid read-only Opus 4.8 max review run (PASS_WITH_FINDINGS) but not closeout-eligible: 1 P2 finding (command-evidence.json empty/pending at review time) and the hardened receipt gate requires zero P2 findings. P2 remediated by executing and recording all pre-closeout validation commands; review re-run and attempt 2 accepted.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-208 (RP06 DMS-core synthetic fixture tail + golden-case entrypoint descriptor pack; risk A; 10 units; RP06.P02.M06.S16-RP06.P02.M07.S03) is a coherent implemented_pending_review pack that does NOT overclaim production readiness: manifest and command-evidence both set production_ready:false, production_ready_flag is a string label, and Hermes emits only production_ready_candidate (gated on coverage+descriptor validity). Read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms the 10 planned units map 1:1 to 10 descriptor-only guards (blocked-claim output, rollback, retry, happy/denied/review unit-test descriptors, integration smoke, service entrypoint contract, request normalization, tenant boundary precheck); deliverable distribution (impl 3 / failure_recovery 2 / test 4 / contract 1) and micro-title counts (Synthetic Fixture Set 7 / Test And Golden Case Set 3) match exactly across baseline, manifest, registry REQUIREMENTS, contract, validators, and tests. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are substantiated: zero filesystem/network/process I/O anywhere in packages/dms/src; freezeCp208Result freezes ~50 false runtime flags; validateDmsCoreCp208SyntheticFixtureTailEntrypointDescriptor asserts every runtime flag false plus all 10 no-leak guards; model.test.js mirrors the assertions; the validate script and contract no_write_attestation agree; README CP00-208 documents the same boundaries. All 7 required_public_exports exist and are re-exported via index.js wildcard. CP178+ hardening holds: forbidden_review_evidence matches the required reject-list (auth_failure, stdout_0_bytes, malformed_json, fenced_json_plus_prose, tool_call_shaped_output, +final-approval/source-mutation/enterprise-trust claims); auth-preflight passed (exit 0, 1212-byte valid JSON, not 0-byte); the captured review output is valid (exit 0, 9210 bytes, parseable, structured_output present with all required fields). Claude is consistently bound as read-only independent reviewer and not final approver; enterprise trust is not claimed from local validation; Hermes patterns appear only as deterministic control-plane evidence/receipt descriptors (gate H06), not embedded product runtime. The prior attempt-1 P2 (empty command-evidence) is remediated — command-evidence now records 20 commands at exit 0, with closeout-pack:validate correctly deferred to the post-review gated step. No P0/P1 defects. Three P3 advisory items remain (post-review artifacts pending on disk; a changed-file-scope double-classification; a stale top-level receipt) — all are expected governance/tooling progression that must complete before commit/closeout but do not block this review. Verdict reflects review-step satisfaction only; human final approval for any enterprise-trust claim remains required and out of scope of this receipt.

findings.title: Three required_pack_artifacts not yet present on disk (expected pending state)
findings.details: manifest.json required_pack_artifacts / evidence_refs list docs/closeout-packs/cp00-208/claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, but only manifest.json and command-evidence.json currently exist in that directory. This is the expected pending state and not an overclaim: production_ready is false, pack_level_claude_review.status is 'pending', and these artifacts are produced by the adjudication, construction_inspection, and receipt steps that follow this read-only review (this review is the input to claude-review-result.json, so it cannot pre-exist).
findings.recommendation: During the subsequent hardening steps, generate claude-review-result.json (from this review), adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, and confirm all five required_pack_artifacts are present before commit/closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Informational; expected output of post-review hardening steps. Verify present before commit/closeout. Does not block this review (no P0/P1).

findings.title: changed-file-scope double-classifies pack untracked files as both implementation and unrelated-dirty-preserved
findings.details: Both entries of untracked_implementation_files (docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md and docs/closeout-packs/cp00-208/) also appear in unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 170-185). Tagging the pack's own evidence directory (docs/closeout-packs/cp00-208/) as 'unrelated' is semantically incorrect since it is the core evidence for THIS pack. Practical impact is nil — active_pack_implementation_refs and git_status_rows correctly capture the 7 modified files plus contract and validate script, and the preserve list only prevents reverts — but the dual/semantically-wrong classification could confuse future scope reconciliation.
findings.recommendation: Reconcile the scope tooling so the pack evidence dir is listed only as an implementation/active untracked file and not under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved; place plan/session bookkeeping (new-session-handoff.md) in a single appropriate bucket.
findings.post_review_disposition: Informational; no effect on pack code correctness. Optional cleanup in scope-generation tooling.

findings.title: Active top-level review-receipt.json is a stale copy of the superseded attempt-01 receipt
findings.details: The top-level review-receipt.json referenced by manifest.review_hardening.receipt_ref and command-evidence.review_hardening.receipt_ref is a verbatim copy of the superseded attempt-01 receipt: same session_id (a33f2329), same normalized_at, closeout_eligible:false, and it still contains the now-remediated P2 (CP00-208-R01 empty command-evidence). Governance state is nonetheless coherent because manifest.pack_level_claude_review.valid_review_receipts is [] and status is 'pending', so closeout is correctly gated and the stale receipt is not counted as valid. A fresh closeout-eligible receipt (p2=0, now that command-evidence is populated) must be produced from this re-run before closeout.
findings.recommendation: Normalize this re-run into a new closeout-eligible review-receipt.json, add it to valid_review_receipts, and confirm closeout_eligible:true with finding_counts p0=p1=p2=0 before advancing to commit.
findings.post_review_disposition: Informational; reflects expected pending re-run state. Must be regenerated from this review prior to closeout. Does not block this review.

adjudication.p2: none_in_accepted_review; attempt-1 P2 fixed by recorded command-execution evidence before re-review
